meeting is being recorded.

Likewise.

I'm going to punish you with three more minutes of me talking.
No problem.
And I don't even know, I don't think Deerage is coming, because he looks like he's double booked on his meeting.
So I'm going to assume he's not coming.
All good.
I think we got an intro from Jay.
And so, you know, it's awesome to connect.

And then happy to do the same if you think would be a good place to start.
Yeah, sure.
Intro about Simplor or about myself or both?
Ah, both.
Yeah, sure.
I mean, so Simpler, I want to say we were like 12 years old ish.
I've been with the company almost nine years.
So I've been here for a long time.
I, you know, recently became the VP of Marketing, although I've been the VP of Growth Marketing for many years here.
So I have a lot of history with the company.
As it relates to where kind of Simpler is, and, you know, I can guess sort of, of why Jay made their introduction.
So, you know, Simpler, you know, is an internet company.
And an internet is not a very  category, but we've done well in the sense that, you know, we're, you know, the top, as it relates to Gartner, Magic Quadrant, and all the other analysts.
And, you know, we think we're the best.
But yeah, I think...
Now there's a couple of things, you know, we're, we're kind of an older startup for lack of a better term, right?
Like we've been around for a little bit.

And, you know, we're building a new platform that's, you know, based on AI, which, you know, it's not going to be a brand new, oh, all of a sudden we're doing, you know, marketing automation or anything like that.
It's still related to employee experience in the digital workplace.
So we're, we're, we're, we're doing a lot of things.
We're, we're trying to be more efficient and effective selling what we are good at right now, while we're making this transition to kind of a new product.
And, you know, Jay, from my understanding, has seen this playbook before and, and I guess thought that you could help us.
So I think that's kind of where we are.
Yeah, that, that, that's perfect.
It's super familiar with the, the space that I heard of you all before.
But, but also like it's.

As things are evolving, you know, I'm sure the product is evolving a ton, but then, you know, the topics and things that your customers care about evolves and, you know, but I'm happy to do a little bit of high level too on my end.
And so I started a career early on at IBM and HP, like large enterprises, and built a lot of those content programs back in the day.

And I think one of the things that I realized was in order for us to win in organic and organic growth overall, we just had to create the best content engine possible and also figure out how to turn organic traffic into distribution.
And that's essentially what I did my whole career.

And then I was at a couple of different companies like ServiceSight and WinPublic, Ashikorp, WinPublic, and then I was the first and only CMO at a company called Skill AI that recently had an exit with Meta and or somewhat of an exit.
And then like...
Like when I was at DeepGram, my last company I was a CMO at, I started building a lot of these things into workflows.
And so these workflows are just like kind of how the thought processes of how to scale content in a really high quality way.
And then I would hire editors in the loop.
And long story short, we 24x are traffic in six months, drove over a million visitors a month organically.
And from there, the company 4x revenue for marketing, and it was a huge inflection curve for the company.
And so I started doing workshops, teaching people how I did that.
And that's how GrowthX got started.
People would just say, hey, you know what, like, I need help with not only building workflows, I need help with the strategy and the execution.
And I don't want to go stitch together a bunch of freelancers, I don't want to go buy a bunch of tools.
And, you know, can you help be an extension of my team?
And that's kind of how GrowthX got started.
And then just a little context, we're now just a little over 70 people, we work with a little over 50.
50 different brands.
So like Ramp and Webflow, Lovable, Engine.com, Augment Code, Reddit, Udemy, like a bunch of like, you know, well-known brands.
And so we're the entire content engine for them, A to Z.
So you can think of us as almost like kind of this AI-powered agency as a way to simplify a bit what we do, you know.
And so for most companies, we're publishing their content daily and scaling it out.
And, and we do a lot more than that, but that's usually like a good starting point to kind of think about, you know.
And so for instance, for you, you have a little over 850 pages on your site.
You know, a lot of pages are part of your glossary and, and your blog, you know.
And so it's ultimately like very similar to the work we're doing with like Abnormal Security or SentinelOne or a few others, you know, where, where, where, we're creating.
Creating that content, you know, and helping them refresh that content and creating that new content.
And I think over the last, like, especially like six months, the way we've seen is kind of how AI answers have just like shifted so much of the attention towards in time towards like answers.

And so a lot of what we do now is like mapping these audiences that you're going after and all the topics they care about and then monitor all those topics and then come up with a lot of content that maps to those topics so that you can be part of those AI answers.
As well, you can increase your brand visibility there.
So, I mean, you know, from at first I was, I thought that you were kind of the, the company that Jay was recommending for kind of SEO, geo, but then I went to your website.
I was like, wait, maybe they do something different.
Yeah, I think our website is probably like evolving quite a bit, but the way to kind of think about it is like expert led in AI powered growth.
So, starting with content.
And so content and distribution and conversion, you know, so.

I think there is some distinction, but not a lot of distinction.
And what I mean by that is like, so just to give you maybe some visuals on, so augment code, like we create these guides for them.
And so these are like pretty technical, but they can be comparisons or informational guides that help them, you know, show up and be cited in a lot of these AI answers.
You know, you can see like multiple articles being cited.
For Webflow, we've done all their integration pages.
For Lovable, we're building a template catalog for them.
For Ramp, we did their entire vendors catalog and we do most of their articles as well in their blog.
We've done a bunch of different places, you know, for town, we do all their text related content.
That is like pretty, pretty technical, but it has to be like really, really accurate as well.
And so it really depends on, on kind of the, the customer.
So I think it varies between really informational top of funnel to all the way to really technical thought leadership, interview based, to programmatic, to newsletters.
And so it really varies from customer to customer, but I'll say the majority of it is somewhere in the top of funnel, middle of funnel, informational content, and thought leadership.
So, but is the content like self-promoting in the sense that it's not like I go like, no, let me go put $50,000 on content a day, syndication.
It's written so that it's captured by the, you know, the, the AI agents and Google.
Is that kind of the secret sauce?
Yeah.
So, so the, way to kind of think about it is, although there are.
are factors that matter more in ADO, then these are some of the factors that we see matter quite a bit.
For instance, content is really well-researched and distributed, comprehensive, up-to-date.

Having authority signals as far as reputable sources that you're mentioning and that you know what you're talking about.
Well-structured, easy to read.
That also happens to be things that are good experiences for your reader.
And so we don't think about it as, like, mutually exclusive of, like, if this is SEO, it's not Geo, because Geo is not for humans.
And if it's for humans, it's not for, it doesn't do well in AI Answers, right?
Like, we try to find where content still needs to build trust with your audience.
And so it can't just be optimizing for machines.
It's like, what will increase your chances of having more visibility than AI Answers while at the same time building trust if someone reads and driving to conversions as well, you know?
So, I'm curious.
I'm if you had enough time to kind of scan what we're doing and kind of identify the areas of growth that we could go after or that you could help us with or, you know.
Yeah, yeah.
Like, so this is, like, a good example here.
Like, this is a piece of content in your glossary, you know, that's doing well, but it, you know, this explains, like, remote work, right?
And so I think, like, this, there's nothing, like, fundamentally wrong with it.

I think you can clearly tell that it's, like, every, every section is two paragraphs and it feels a little bit robotic and, you know, it doesn't mention your product whatsoever and we're here, actually.
Or if it does, it's, like, towards here and it doesn't, it's not as nuanced, right?
To, like, simpler, for example, integrates with blah, blah, blah, but it doesn't mention, like,

Like, so something like that, right?
Like, where maybe you would have, like, a lot more context.
Hopefully, maybe you show up, but maybe you don't.
I have done this before, so I don't know.
And so our goal is to make sure you show up here at the very top of this, you know?
And, but not only with these types of, like, high, like, very transactional comparisons or bottom of funnel, those are great.
And that's usually where we start.
But you also want to do this with things that are more informational.
So, for instance.

It's like if you search for, let's just say, what expense category is office furniture, and you're trying to sort through a bunch of data, you know, credit card data, and you want your first search result to be hopefully your brand.
So when you click on this, it's like actually a page that we generated for brand, right?

And then you're ranking well.

And then from there, we create this content OS of all the opportunities we want to execute against.
And then long term, how...

Like, so think of this as like, an agentic researcher, an article drafter, and they have an assistant that is context aware that our editors can use to be able to, you know, expand on things, or if they want to just edit this and say, know what?
So is this your proprietary AI that's written this?
Correct.
Yeah.
With an expert in the loop, and this is like an agentic workflow, right?
Like, so in this example of a workflow, this is like, like,

And then I need to evaluate the output for hallucination, accurately supported, potential issues found.
And then in the next run, it went through and it scored 0.75 and here's the issues.

Yeah, so, and I guess kind of as it relates to this right now, so my, like, we basically use a combination of a few things, right?

Yeah.

So she thinks, you know, content first and SEO second, whereas the writers in India are SEO first and then content second, if that makes sense.
Got it.

Yeah, we don't have freelancers.
They're all employees.
Employees.

Our agency?

No.

And we need to change these pages because this is set up wrong and whatever, whatever, whatever.
Our internal writers use Quarter, the software, to basically tell us how, like what to write about and how to write it so that it ranks.
That makes sense.
Yeah.

So I want to say quarter somewhere between $30,000 and $40,000 a year.
So the software is like $3,000 a month, say our agency is $3,000.
Yeah.
Um, and, um, I would say, like, so, so our, our India folks, you know, you can guess that it's, let's say it's one and a half people, so it's 60 grand for salary.
And then folks here, let's just say it's a quarter of a person, or let's just say it's 50% of a person, right, because they don't all do SEO.

So that's, so that's, you know, whatever it is, 150, 200 grand on salary, and then $3,000 on software, $3,000 on an agency per month.
Um, and they're probably generating 10, 20, 20 blogs a month, right?
Like, some of it's more, like, versus writing new content.
Got it.
That, that makes sense.
And then, like, overall, like, when you think about, like, your organic traffic, like, do you feel like...
It's working well.
Yeah, I mean, what I find is, if there's a keyword we're targeting, we can get to number one.
Like, we know how to get to number one.
So you have that benefit, right?
And then in AI ensures, like, do you have a good sense of, like, how well you're doing in the...
Yeah, so, like, whether it's SEO, SEO is the hardest, I would say, but whether it's SEO or AI overviews or Perplexer or ChatGBT, if the goal is, you know, top two, we can get there.
Got it.
Yeah.
Got it.
And so, yeah, so, like, maybe one way to kind of think about us a little bit more is, like, we would do all the things you just described.
And with most teams, like, for instance, with Ramp, they still have, like, four content people internally that, excuse me, we work with.

And then, like, so the way we kind of think about it is, like, we're, like, you have essentially 800-plus pages that you need to refresh a lot more often, like, every three months, ideally.
And you also want to show up when people search for things like this, right, in AI answers, right?
Like, so it sounds like, I think maybe, like, your SEO, like, you're here, but I think, like, the citation here is actually, like, not you being cited here.
you're to

Hopefully that helps a little bit, but I think it's like, I would say we would overlap quite a bit and not be necessary to have an agency working on it.

And then.

So I think it's mostly like the way to think about it, it's more about the technical complexity and the right strategy based on your audience.
Like for some of our more technical customers where, you know, this stuff has to be highly, highly vetted and checked.

But like companies like that, like for instance, like town.com, like where we've done their blogs, like we can go higher volume as well, you know, even though it's like really, really like technical as well.
And so it really depends.

But, but, but that's a little bit of the, the high level, but totally understand also, it's like the way I would think about it is like, if it's.

it's not broken and you're showing up in all AI answers and your content is fresh and really high quality and the setup is working with agencies, freelancers, and tools or agency internal teams and tools, then usually it's like, hey, you figured it out.
And so that's good.

I have a high quality way to scale content and traffic, you know, so we can share some more details as well as the recording of this and then some, a ton of customer examples and some slides too.
Yeah, and I know we're out of time.

So we are hamstrung.

Yeah.

Right.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Explore potential collaboration between Simpplr and GrowthX for AI-powered content creation and SEO optimization.](https://fathom.video/share/y6rW8gruomeoUExi2jkwHjkWyFu4X1M7?tab=summary&timestamp=2.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [GrowthX offers AI-powered content creation with expert editors, focusing on SEO and AI answer visibility](https://fathom.video/share/y6rW8gruomeoUExi2jkwHjkWyFu4X1M7?tab=summary&timestamp=170.0)
  - [Simpplr currently uses a mix of tools (Quarter), agency guidance, and internal writers for content/SEO](https://fathom.video/share/y6rW8gruomeoUExi2jkwHjkWyFu4X1M7?tab=summary&timestamp=972.0)
  - [GrowthX's services ($9k-$15k/mo) could potentially replace or augment Simpplr's current setup (\~$200k/yr)](https://fathom.video/share/y6rW8gruomeoUExi2jkwHjkWyFu4X1M7?tab=summary&timestamp=1233.0)
  - [Technical/IT-focused content is a key area of interest for Simpplr, aligning with GrowthX's expertise](https://fathom.video/share/y6rW8gruomeoUExi2jkwHjkWyFu4X1M7?tab=summary&timestamp=1643.0)

## Topics

### Simpplr's Current Content/SEO Strategy

  - [Using Quarter ($30k-$40k/yr) for SEO insights](https://fathom.video/share/y6rW8gruomeoUExi2jkwHjkWyFu4X1M7?tab=summary&timestamp=1130.0)
  - [SEO agency ($3k/mo) provides keyword and optimization guidance](https://fathom.video/share/y6rW8gruomeoUExi2jkwHjkWyFu4X1M7?tab=summary&timestamp=1135.0)
  - [Internal writers: 1.5 FTE in India (\~$60k/yr), 0.5 FTE in US (\~$70k/yr)](https://fathom.video/share/y6rW8gruomeoUExi2jkwHjkWyFu4X1M7?tab=summary&timestamp=1140.0)
  - [Output: 10-20 blogs/month, mix of new content and optimization](https://fathom.video/share/y6rW8gruomeoUExi2jkwHjkWyFu4X1M7?tab=summary&timestamp=1425.0)
  - Successfully achieve \#1 rankings for targeted keywords
  - [Strong performance in AI overviews (e.g., Perplexity, ChatGPT)](https://fathom.video/share/y6rW8gruomeoUExi2jkwHjkWyFu4X1M7?tab=summary&timestamp=1308.0)

### GrowthX's Offering

  - [AI-powered content creation with expert editors in the loop](https://fathom.video/share/y6rW8gruomeoUExi2jkwHjkWyFu4X1M7?tab=summary&timestamp=830.0)
  - [Proprietary platform for content strategy, creation, and performance tracking](https://fathom.video/share/y6rW8gruomeoUExi2jkwHjkWyFu4X1M7?tab=summary&timestamp=780.0)
  - [Daily publishing (12-15k words/mo), high-volume content refreshes](https://fathom.video/share/y6rW8gruomeoUExi2jkwHjkWyFu4X1M7?tab=summary&timestamp=1444.0)
  - [Comprehensive service: research, writing, fact-checking, graphics, publishing, optimization](https://fathom.video/share/y6rW8gruomeoUExi2jkwHjkWyFu4X1M7?tab=summary&timestamp=1356.0)
  - [8-week strategy sprint ($20k) followed by ongoing execution ($9k-$15k/mo)](https://fathom.video/share/y6rW8gruomeoUExi2jkwHjkWyFu4X1M7?tab=summary&timestamp=1707.0)
  - [Expertise in technical content (e.g., IT, security, ITSM)](https://fathom.video/share/y6rW8gruomeoUExi2jkwHjkWyFu4X1M7?tab=summary&timestamp=1660.0)

### Content Strategy and AI Visibility

  - [Focus on creating comprehensive, well-researched, up-to-date content](https://fathom.video/share/y6rW8gruomeoUExi2jkwHjkWyFu4X1M7?tab=summary&timestamp=548.0)
  - [Emphasis on authority signals and reputable sources](https://fathom.video/share/y6rW8gruomeoUExi2jkwHjkWyFu4X1M7?tab=summary&timestamp=556.0)
  - [Structured for both SEO and reader experience](https://fathom.video/share/y6rW8gruomeoUExi2jkwHjkWyFu4X1M7?tab=summary&timestamp=561.0)
  - [Aim to increase visibility in AI answers (e.g., Perplexity, ChatGPT)](https://fathom.video/share/y6rW8gruomeoUExi2jkwHjkWyFu4X1M7?tab=summary&timestamp=699.0)
